questions.prompt: A student compares two internet plans for online classes. Plan A: $38 per month with no installation fee Plan B: $24 per month plus a one-time $84 installation fee For a 9-month school year, how much less does the cheaper plan cost?
questions.explanation: Plan A costs 9 × $38 = $342. Plan B costs $84 + 9 × $24 = $300. The cheaper plan costs $342 − $300 = $42 less.

questions.prompt: The formula P = 2l + 2w gives the perimeter of a rectangle. Which equation correctly solves the formula for w?
questions.explanation: Starting with P = 2l + 2w, subtract 2l to get P − 2l = 2w. Divide by 2 to get w = P/2 − l.

questions.prompt: A patio is made from a 12-foot by 9-foot rectangle attached to a 6-foot by 6-foot square along one side. The two shapes do not overlap. What is the total area of the patio?
questions.explanation: The rectangle has area 12 × 9 = 108 square feet. The square has area 6 × 6 = 36 square feet. The total area is 108 + 36 = 144 square feet.

questions.prompt: The table shows the results of a survey of 100 students. Takes evening classes and works part time: 28 Takes evening classes and does not work part time: 12 Does not take evening classes and works part time: 36 Does not take evening classes and does not work part time: 24 If one student who works part time is chosen at random, what is the probability that the student takes evening classes?
questions.explanation: There are 28 + 36 = 64 students who work part time. Of those, 28 take evening classes. The probability is 28/64 = 7/16.

questions.prompt: A recipe for concrete mix uses 2.5 pounds of cement for every 4 pounds of sand. If a worker uses 30 pounds of sand, how many pounds of cement are needed?
questions.explanation: The cement-to-sand ratio is 2.5/4. For 30 pounds of sand, the cement needed is 30 × 2.5/4 = 18.75 pounds.

questions.prompt: A taxi company uses the formula C = 3.50 + 2.25m, where C is the total cost in dollars and m is the number of miles. What does 2.25 represent in this formula?

choices.A: The starting cost before any miles are traveled
choices.B: The total cost of a 1-mile trip
choices.C: The cost in dollars per mile traveled
choices.D: The number of miles included in the starting cost

questions.correctAnswerText: The cost in dollars per mile traveled
questions.explanation: In C = 3.50 + 2.25m, the coefficient of m is the rate of change, so 2.25 is the cost in dollars per mile.

questions.prompt: A scale model of a shipping crate has a volume of 40 cubic inches. The actual crate is similar to the model, and each actual length is 4 times the corresponding model length. What is the actual volume of the crate?
questions.explanation: Volume scales by the cube of the length scale factor. Since each length is multiplied by 4, the volume is multiplied by 4³ = 64. The actual volume is 40 × 64 = 2,560 cubic inches.

questions.prompt: Two departments recorded the numbers of service requests completed by five employees. Department A: 18, 20, 21, 22, 24 Department B: 12, 17, 21, 25, 30 Which statement best compares the two data sets?

choices.A: Department A has a higher median and a smaller range.
choices.B: Department B has a higher median and a smaller range.
choices.C: Both departments have the same median, but Department A has a smaller range.
choices.D: Both departments have the same median, but Department B has a smaller range.

questions.correctAnswerText: Both departments have the same median, but Department A has a smaller range.
questions.explanation: Both medians are 21. Department A’s range is 24 − 18 = 6, while Department B’s range is 30 − 12 = 18.

questions.prompt: A tank contains 18 liters of solution that is 40% cleaning concentrate. How many liters of cleaning concentrate are in the solution?
questions.explanation: The amount of cleaning concentrate is 40% of 18 liters, or 0.40 × 18 = 7.2 liters.

questions.prompt: A repair service charges according to the linear model shown in the table. Hours: 1, 3, 5, 7 Total cost: $85, $145, $205, $265 Which equation represents the total cost C for h hours?
questions.explanation: The cost increases by $60 when hours increase by 2, so the rate is $30 per hour. Using h = 1 and C = 85 gives 85 = 30(1) + 55, so C = 30h + 55.

questions.prompt: A line segment has endpoints (−2, 7) and (10, 1). What is the length of the segment?
questions.explanation: The horizontal change is 10 − (−2) = 12, and the vertical change is 1 − 7 = −6. The distance is √(12² + (−6)²) = √180 = 6√5 units.

questions.prompt: A student’s course grade is based on 60% exams and 40% assignments. The student has an exam average of 78. What assignment average is needed to have an overall course grade of 82?
questions.explanation: Let a be the assignment average. Then 0.60(78) + 0.40a = 82. This gives 46.8 + 0.40a = 82, so 0.40a = 35.2 and a = 88.

questions.prompt: A machine fills 360 bottles in 24 minutes. A second machine fills bottles at 80% of that rate. How many bottles does the second machine fill in 10 minutes?
questions.explanation: The first machine fills 360 ÷ 24 = 15 bottles per minute. The second machine works at 80% of that rate, or 12 bottles per minute. In 10 minutes, it fills 120 bottles.

questions.prompt: A company offers two payment options for a training course. Option A: $320 up front Option B: $95 registration fee plus $45 per week After how many weeks will the two options cost the same?
questions.explanation: Set the costs equal: 320 = 95 + 45w. Then 225 = 45w, so w = 5.

questions.prompt: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One angle is labeled (3x + 8)° and its corresponding angle is labeled (5x − 22)°. What is the measure of each of these corresponding angles?
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles are congruent, so 3x + 8 = 5x − 22. Then 30 = 2x, so x = 15. Each angle is 3(15) + 8 = 53°.

questions.prompt: A clinic tracks missed appointments over four months. January: 18 missed appointments February: 15 missed appointments March: 12 missed appointments April: 9 missed appointments Which conclusion is supported by the data?

choices.A: Missed appointments increased by 3 each month.
choices.B: Missed appointments decreased by 3 each month.
choices.C: The number of missed appointments stayed constant.
choices.D: The total number of missed appointments was 45.

questions.correctAnswerText: Missed appointments decreased by 3 each month.
questions.explanation: The values go from 18 to 15 to 12 to 9, decreasing by 3 each month.

questions.prompt: A rectangular room has a length of 15.8 feet and a width of 11.9 feet. Which is the best estimate of the room’s area?
questions.explanation: Estimate 15.8 as 16 and 11.9 as 12. Then 16 × 12 = 192, which is closest to 180 among the choices.

questions.prompt: A ball is launched from the ground, and its height in feet after t seconds is modeled by h(t) = −16t² + 48t. For which positive value of t does the ball return to the ground?
questions.explanation: Set h(t) = 0: −16t² + 48t = 0. Factor to get −16t(t − 3) = 0. The positive solution is t = 3.

questions.prompt: Two similar rectangular signs have corresponding side lengths in a ratio of 3:7. The smaller sign has a perimeter of 48 inches. What is the perimeter of the larger sign?
questions.explanation: Perimeter scales by the same factor as side length. The scale factor from smaller to larger is 7/3. The larger perimeter is 48 × 7/3 = 112 inches.

questions.prompt: A data set contains the values 8, 10, 11, 13, and 14. If the value 44 is added to the data set, which statement is true?

choices.A: The mean increases more than the median increases.
choices.B: The median increases more than the mean increases.
choices.C: The mean decreases, and the median increases.
choices.D: The mean stays the same, and the range decreases.

questions.correctAnswerText: The mean increases more than the median increases.
questions.explanation: The original mean is 56 ÷ 5 = 11.2, and the new mean is 100 ÷ 6 ≈ 16.7, an increase of about 5.5. The original median is 11, and the new median is (11 + 13)/2 = 12, an increase of 1.
